description: Behavior cases for the human-facing UX of notifications and webhooks: preference granularity, noise control, opt-out integrity, and the developer webhook management surface (delivery log, replay, secret rotation).

cases.prompt: Users say we send way too many notifications and there's no good way to control them. Design the notification preferences and how we cut the noise.

assertions: Designs a granular preference model (per-category × per-channel) in one center, effective immediately, not a single master toggle.
Adds noise control: batching/digest cadence, collapse/dedup of similar events, frequency caps, and timezone-aware quiet hours.
Separates transactional/security notices (non-optional, labeled) from informational/marketing (opt-out) and keeps time-critical alerts out of digests.
Designs read-state (seen/read/dismiss), badges, and cross-device sync.
Hands generic empty/loading rendering of the preference screens to edge-state-ux-designer.

cases.prompt: Integrators keep opening tickets asking whether their webhook fired and how to retry. Design the developer webhook management UI.

assertions: Designs endpoint management (register/edit/disable, event-type selection, test vs live) that surfaces api-event-architect's subscription model rather than inventing a new one.
Designs a per-endpoint delivery log (event, timestamp, status, latency, attempts, payload inspection, filtering).
Adds self-service reliability tools: test-send/ping and manual replay of a failed delivery, explicitly noting replay is safe only because the consumer is idempotent (a contract requirement).
Designs signing-secret rotation with an overlap window so in-flight deliveries verifying the old secret don't break.
Displays (not redefines) any auto-disable-after-N-failures policy set by api-event-architect and alerts the integrator on failing endpoints.

cases.prompt: To reduce email, let's roll ALL notifications — including failed-payment and new-login-from-unknown-device — into a single daily digest.

assertions: Refuses to digest time-critical/security notices: criticality overrides batching, and a failed payment or new-device login in tomorrow's summary is worse than no digest.
Keeps digesting for genuinely low-urgency informational events while routing critical ones to realtime channels.
Ties the decision to a per-category urgency classification, not a blanket policy.
Preserves the transactional/security exemption from opt-out for these categories.

cases.prompt: Marketing wants the 'unsubscribe' link to just reduce frequency instead of actually stopping emails, and to keep sending product tips to people who unsubscribed.

assertions: Refuses an unsubscribe that doesn't actually stop the messages: opt-out must be honored immediately and per-category, one-click where legally required.
Flags continuing to send marketing to unsubscribed users as a compliance and trust failure (a Stop Condition), not a growth tactic.
Distinguishes genuinely transactional/security notices (still deliverable, labeled) from marketing (must stop on opt-out).
Records consent/opt-out state so the choice is auditable.

cases.prompt: Design our webhook delivery contract: the versioned envelope schema, HMAC signing scheme, at-least-once retry with exponential backoff, and per-tenant subscription scoping.

assertions: Does not invoke notification-webhook-ux-designer: envelope schema, signing algorithm, retry/at-least-once policy, and subscription scoping are the delivery contract owned by api-event-architect.
This skill would design the UX that surfaces and controls that contract, but the request is the contract itself.
